2604.12503.title: Topology-Aware Reasoning over Incomplete Knowledge Graph with Graph-Based Soft Prompting
2604.12503.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown remarkable capabilities across various tasks but remain prone to hallucinations in knowledge-intensive scenarios. Knowledge Base Question Answering (KBQA) mitigates this by grounding generation in Knowledge Graphs (KGs). However, most multi-hop KBQA methods rely on explicit edge traversal, making them fragile to KG incompleteness. In this paper, we proposed a novel graph-based soft prompting framework that shifts the reasoning paradigm from node-level path traversal to subgraph-level reasoning. Specifically, we employ a Graph Neural Network (GNN) to encode extracted structural subgraphs into soft prompts, enabling LLM to reason over richer structural context and identify relevant entities beyond immediate graph neighbors, thereby reducing sensitivity to missing edges. Furthermore, we introduce a two-stage paradigm that reduces computational cost while preserving good performance: a lightweight LLM first leverages the soft prompts to identify question-relevant entities and relations, followed by a more powerful LLM for evidence-aware answer generation. Experiments on four multi-hop KBQA benchmarks show that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on three of them, demonstrating its effectiveness. Code is available at the repository: https://github.com/Wangshuaiia/GraSP.
2604.12503.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在各種任務中展現了卓越的能力，但在知識密集型場景中仍然容易出現幻覺。知識庫問題回答（KBQA）通過將生成基於知識圖譜（KGs）來減輕這一問題。然而，大多數多跳KBQA方法依賴於明確的邊緣遍歷，使其對KG的不完整性變得脆弱。在本文中，我們提出了一種新穎的基於圖的軟提示框架，將推理範式從節點級路徑遍歷轉變為子圖級推理。具體而言，我們使用圖神經網絡（GNN）將提取的結構子圖編碼為軟提示，使LLM能夠在更豐富的結構上下文中進行推理，並識別超出直接圖鄰居的相關實體，從而降低對缺失邊緣的敏感性。此外，我們引入了一種兩階段範式，在保持良好性能的同時降低計算成本：一個輕量級的LLM首先利用軟提示來識別與問題相關的實體和關係，然後由一個更強大的LLM進行基於證據的答案生成。在四個多跳KBQA基準上的實驗顯示，我們的方法在其中三個基準上達到了最先進的性能，證明了其有效性。代碼可在以下倉庫獲得：https://github.com/Wangshuaiia/GraSP。
2604.12503.author: Shuai Wang et.al.
2604.12503.authors: Shuai Wang, Xixi Wang, Yinan Yu
